_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.639), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.615).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.904 (P=0.950, R=0.863). P exceeds R by 0.088 — the DCWF element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Skill in technical writing." and "Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical aud" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.640 ≈ STS 0.611 (gap 0.029). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [skill, technical, writing] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 21.1%. Shared tokens: [skill, technical, writing]. DCWF-only: []. WIDAI-only: [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats].
